title: pythagorea virgata
intro: Pythagorea virgata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Aster family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the "Milk Thistle" due to its milky sap. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain silymarin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Pythagorea virgata, such as capsules and teas, are used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, as well as to improve digestion and protect against liver damage, with potential health benefits including reduced risk of liver disease and improved overall well-being.
